steps.title: Identify the grammatical subject
steps.content: The subject is the singular noun "analysis" (head of the long noun phrase "James Melton and Tom Ginsburg's analysis of de jure judicial independence and its growth over decades"). The possessors and the prepositional modifiers do not change the head noun's number.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content: A singular subject needs a singular present-tense verb. "Identifies" is the singular present-tense form and fits the ongoing, factual claim about what the analysis does.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'are identifying': plural verb form, violates subject-verb agreement with singular "analysis." ✗
  • Choice B — 'identify': plural present-tense form, violates subject-verb agreement. ✗
  • Choice C — 'have identified': plural present-perfect form ("have"), violates subject-verb agreement. ✗
  • Choice D — 'identifies': singular present-tense form, agrees with singular subject "analysis." ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
